displayName: Progressive Enhancement — Caching, Charts, Search, Notifications, Forecasting

decisions.decision: React.cache() for per-request dedup + cacheTag/cacheLife for cross-request caching
decisions.rationale: Eliminates 11 duplicate getHouseholdId() implementations and double-fetch in layout. No external cache needed — in-process only. revalidateTag() replaces all revalidatePath() calls for surgical invalidation.

decisions.decision: Extract single getAuthContext() with React.cache() returning { user, householdId, supabase }
decisions.rationale: One auth call per request instead of N. All 11 action files import from shared module. Layout uses same cached context.

decisions.decision: Supabase Realtime subscription in (app)/layout.tsx for household-scoped cache invalidation
decisions.rationale: When household member A edits data, member B's cached pages get invalidated via revalidateTag(). Thin client component wrapper in layout subscribes to household channel.

decisions.decision: Recharts library replacing all 4 hand-rolled chart components
decisions.rationale: Hand-rolled CSS charts lack tooltips, interactivity, and chart type variety. Recharts is the user's preferred library. Server components pass data, thin client wrappers render charts.

decisions.decision: New report pages: monthly trends (LineChart), income vs expenses (BarChart), category breakdown (PieChart)
decisions.rationale: Recharts enables proper report visualizations. Reports are server-rendered with data from cached server actions.

decisions.decision: pg_trgm extension with GIN index on transactions.notes for fuzzy text search
decisions.rationale: Supabase built-in, no external search engine needed at household scale. Uses similarity() or ILIKE with trigram index for fast fuzzy matching.

decisions.decision: Inline search bar on transactions page with real-time filtering
decisions.rationale: Simpler than command palette for initial implementation. Server action performs search, results update in-place. Can add Cmd+K later.

decisions.decision: notifications table in Supabase (id, household_id, user_id, type, title, body, read, created_at)
decisions.rationale: Standard notification pattern. Scoped to household, read status per user. Realtime subscription for live updates.

decisions.decision: pg_cron nightly job → Supabase Edge Function → check overdue bills & budget overspend → insert notification + email via Resend
decisions.rationale: No background worker needed. pg_cron runs in Supabase, Edge Function handles logic. Nightly batch is sufficient for bill/budget alerts. Resend free tier (100 emails/day) covers household use.

decisions.decision: Notification bell in sidebar with unread count, dropdown list with mark-as-read
decisions.rationale: Standard UX pattern. Realtime subscription updates count without polling. Bell badge shows unread count.

decisions.decision: Pure server-side computation — projected balances, budget runway, bill calendar
decisions.rationale: No background processing needed. Projected balance = sum scheduled income - scheduled expenses over N months. Budget runway = remaining / daily rate. Bill calendar = query recurring items + project dates.

decisions.decision: Stay on Vercel + Supabase, no Docker/Redis/workers
decisions.rationale: Progressive Enhancement philosophy — use what Supabase already provides (pg_trgm, pg_cron, Edge Functions, Realtime). Clean upgrade path to self-hosted (Option B) if needed later.

constraints: Performance first — caching and faster loads are top priority (Phase 1)
No new infrastructure — leverage Supabase built-ins only
Existing stack: Next.js 16, React 19, Supabase, Tailwind 4
Supabase free-tier Edge Function limits — mitigate with nightly batch
pg_cron granularity is minutes, not seconds — acceptable for bill/budget alerts
Must not break existing features during migration
openQuestions: Which Supabase tables should trigger Realtime subscriptions? (start with transactions, budgets, bills)
Should search extend beyond transactions.notes to include category names and account names?
Email notification opt-in/opt-out — per-user setting or household-level?
Forecasting time horizon — default 3 months or configurable?

phases.name: Cache + Shared Auth
phases.scope: Extract getAuthContext() with React.cache(), add cacheTag/cacheLife to read actions, revalidateTag on mutations, Realtime listener in layout

phases.name: Recharts + Reports
phases.scope: Install Recharts, replace 4 hand-rolled charts, add monthly/yearly report pages

phases.name: Search + Filters
phases.scope: Enable pg_trgm, GIN index on transactions.notes, search server action + search UI on transactions page

phases.name: Notifications
phases.scope: notifications table, pg_cron nightly job, Edge Function for email (Resend), notification bell in sidebar with Realtime

phases.name: Forecasting
phases.scope: Projected balance calculator, budget runway, bill calendar view (30/60/90 days)
